title: Chapter Seventy-Seven: White Qilin
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

He saw the crescent moon and counted.

Fifteen days. In his heart it felt closer to a hundred. What could the small piece mean? Between himself and Desolate Sect's lord, it could only be one thing.

*The World Tree's fruit.*

He suppressed the thought each time it surfaced. Stamped it down the way one stamps out a grass fire. It was a life spent rushing toward that fruit. He was afraid of hope. Hope could wound as deeply as any blade.

*Don't believe. Not yet.*

He walked to the General Administration Office with Won-chang at his shoulder. The clerk managing the entry register welcomed him with a familiar nod.

"You've come to volunteer for the Namgung family martial matches?"

"I have."

"I'll record it. Though the notice was only just posted, many warriors are already putting their names down." The clerk sighed. "This is the first time a mission assignment has been this fierce."

"Are there that many volunteers?" Won-chang asked, leaning forward.

"Young Hero Lightning Genius should properly keep the competition in mind."

A clear voice came from behind.

"It's because the Namgung supreme masters are that powerful. To win all martial matches, shouldn't the mission be composed of personnel selected from each martial power?"

Yeon-shin had already turned. He knew that voice.

The blue robe of the man walking steadily toward them fluttered magnificently. His jawline was elegant. His pitch-black pupils frequently flashed with light. Fair skin, high nose bridge -- a peerless beautiful youth whose presence alone was enough to still a room.

"Young Hero Namgung."

Namgung Hwa-sin returned the clasped-hands greeting immediately. "Young Hero Jeong. It's truly been a long time." His smile was genuine. Open. The kind of smile that belonged to someone who had been raised in comfort, who had never needed to learn the careful blankness that Yeon-shin wore like armour. "Feels like just yesterday we took the Desolate Sect test together."

*He's different.*

Yeon-shin felt it at once. The energy waves rising from deep within Hwa-sin's body were extraordinary -- profound, dense, and heavy with a power that had not been there before. Whatever spirit medicines he had dissolved since leaving the Namgung family, they had transformed him.

*Jin Ju-rim was right. Two moves.*

Women from Obeying Heaven Wing surrounded Hwa-sin -- four of them, each armed, each radiating refined energy. Among them, a white-ranked master with silver ornaments pinned in braided hair stepped forward.

"Are you Radiant Demon Wing's Lightning Genius? I've heard much about you. I am Shin Bin-bin."

Yeon-shin clasped his hands politely. She evaluated him openly, then turned to Hwa-sin with a smile that was not quite kind.

"Senior Namgung's path is lonely. Though this one seems like he could be a companion, I'm not sure."

Yeon-shin stopped Won-chang from stepping forward with a touch on his arm. He could see the muttering beginning on those lips. Not worth the trouble.

"Did Young Hero Namgung also come to volunteer for this mission?" Yeon-shin asked. His eyes were on Hwa-sin alone.

"Though I left, this body's roots are in South Zhili." Hwa-sin's expression mixed bitterness and apology -- even that was like a painting. "Since there are matters to settle with the family, it's right that I step forward. I have no face to say anything to Young Hero Jeong. Even apologising on behalf of the family's behaviour seems presumptuous."

"What my mental strength focuses on now," Yeon-shin said slowly, "is only Young Hero Namgung's martial might as a competition opponent."

Hwa-sin held his gaze. Something passed between them -- recognition.

*You understand,* Yeon-shin thought. *We are the same kind of creature.*

---

Three days passed. Twelve until the full moon.

The General Administration Office announced the competition brackets. Two matches per day, observed by masters from other martial powers. Winners advancing.

White Qilin Namgung Hwa-sin cut through the bracket like a blade through silk. Unstoppable. No match among those his age. The masters watching gave uniform impressions:

"Very solid. No weakness. He built his energy techniques properly."

"Did the Namgung family pour that much spirit medicine even into an illegitimate son? His power is tremendous."

Yeon-shin's evaluation was different. He showed increasingly stronger aspects as days passed -- healing internal injuries even while fighting. All masters agreed his true qi operation was incomparably delicate.

And Hwa-sin watched. Every one of Yeon-shin's matches. Taking in each technique, each verse, each unique martial art as it was revealed. Radiant Wing Step, Eternal Blossom Fist, Radiant Sword Style -- all of it engraved in White Qilin's eyes.

*I know my move forms have been analysed.*

A master of Hwa-sin's level would have prepared countermeasures. Knowing the enemy and knowing oneself -- Namgung Hwa-sin had chosen to know his enemy.

Meanwhile, Yeon-shin focused on knowing himself.

*The martial art that can trigger the biggest single strike is Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm. But it's a palm technique -- cannot be used with a sword. I need Eternal Blossom Fist.*

Three moves. Spiral. Advancing Thunder. Blossom Fist. Rotation, hard fist, change.

*Create out of necessity.*

---

Finally, two remained. The final preliminary.

Namgung Hwa-sin landed in the practice ground's centre from above, his Unhindered Clear Flow Body carrying him across the sky as if the air itself were a road. His blue robe settled around him like water finding its level.

"The martial arena where we met at Desolate City was modest," Hwa-sin said. "This place is quite grand. Many are watching."

"I understand there are deep circumstances in your decision to volunteer," Yeon-shin replied. "Forgive my inability to yield."

"Wouldn't you have such circumstances too?"

"Let's share our thoughts later. Won't you go together to South Zhili?"

Hwa-sin laughed -- a genuine sound, bright as his sword. "Sharing stories on the way to cut down my brother. That would have quite the charm."

"Begin," the Annihilation Team Leader said.

The clear ring of Hwa-sin's drawn sword filled the training ground. Yeon-shin's hands moved -- right hand to the Desolate Sword, left hand open, palm turned toward the sky.

*Now.*

He raised opposing formulas in his left hand. Demon Roar Blood Energy and Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm -- powers that repelled each other without neutralising. The sound was loud. Demon whispers mixed with stream wind.

He spread his palm.

BANG.

Energy waves exploded. Compressed air hammered down to his feet. Dust shot backwards. Hwa-sin's body bounced -- seven steps back from the shockwave alone.

*The fourth move.* Incomplete. Raw. But real.

In the tiny instant Hwa-sin regained his balance, Yeon-shin planted one foot. True qi burst from the Bubbling Well point. He closed five steps in one. Drew the Desolate Sword.

*Radiant Sword Style.*

The horizontal strike met Hwa-sin's Azure Sky Boundless Sword with a clash that sent colorless energy sweeping across the training ground. Their eyes met over locked blades. Hwa-sin was completely immersed.

Then the pressure came. Deep internal energy, pushing, overwhelming. The sword force grew stronger. Yeon-shin's arm trembled.

*He overwhelms with force where technique cannot decide it. This is what knowing the enemy means.*

He thought of the spirit pills he had deliberately not consumed. He would face this disadvantage again and again -- enemies raised on medicines since birth, their muscles and veins saturated with power he could not match.

*Blaming internal energy each time would be foolish.*

He spun. Yielded in the power struggle deliberately. Loaded Battle Vision into his left fist -- Eternal Blossom Fist, Spiral -- and drove it into Hwa-sin's flank.

A thud. Hwa-sin's body technique was exquisite -- he spun with the blow, his robe catching in the energy waves before spreading out again like a bird's wing.

*Shallow. I couldn't reach the organs.*

And there -- Hwa-sin's counter had cut his thigh. Stinging. One full exchange.

They advanced again. Evasion and collision repeating endlessly.

CLANG. CLANG. THUD.

He unleashed everything. Radiant Sword Style, Radiant Wing Step, Eternal Blossom Fist -- all three moves, all variations. He made this ground a forge for refining martial arts. Even if blood sprayed. Even if sword marks opened wounds.

The crowd fell silent. It was a martial match between geniuses. Brilliance flashed in the endless exchange. The clamour died to whispers.

"Truly marvelous..."

"Endless unknown martial arts, yet not losing to the Eight Great Families' ancient divine techniques..."

Yeon-shin heard none of it. He was immersed. They exchanged sense, move battles, martial art essentials -- a realm of qualities shining like comets.

*I wish it would continue forever.*

There was a feeling of the unique martial arts seeping through his entire body. His techniques rose endlessly more precise. The sensation of talents clashing and rising was intoxicating.

Then -- something shifted.

The anxiety about the full moon, the fear, the desperate hope -- it all fell away. He recalled spirit pills. He would soon meet Desolate Sect's lord. The future opened before him, bright and burning.

The boy began to smile.

---
